stories.title: LeBron James Day 4 — Heat Enters Super Team Talk

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Day 4 and LeBron James still hasn't signed. The Heat are now a serious contender — Giannis + Bam + LeBron in Miami? The NBA world is watching. No deal until July 6. #NBA #LeBronJames #Heat

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: LeBron is 41 and still commanding max attention across the NBA. Warriors (-500 yesterday), Heat, and Cavs all in the mix. Rich Paul is taking calls. Decision expected July 6. Where does he land? #NBA #LeBronJames

facebook.long_form:
We are four days into NBA free agency and LeBron James still has not made his decision — and the league is better for it.

The newest wrinkle in the LeBron sweepstakes? The Miami Heat are now a legitimate, serious contender. Miami recently acquired Giannis Antetokounmpo in a blockbuster deal, and they already have Bam Adebayo anchoring their frontcourt. The pitch to LeBron James is simple and staggering: come form the most star-studded super team the NBA has ever seen. Giannis. Bam. The King. In Miami.

The Golden State Warriors remain the betting favorites — they were listed at -500 yesterday — and the Cleveland Cavaliers (+300) still represent the most emotionally resonant option. LeBron delivered Cleveland its only NBA championship in 2016, one of the greatest moments in basketball history. The idea of a final homecoming is something Cavaliers fans are dreaming about right now.

What makes this saga so compelling is LeBron's flexibility. At 41 years old, he is reportedly willing to accept the Mid-Level Exception or even a veteran minimum to land in the right situation. This is not about money. This is about one final championship run. Rich Paul is taking calls. All decisions are on hold until July 6, when the moratorium lifts at 12:01 PM ET. The whole basketball world is watching.

**Which destination would you choose if you were LeBron? Drop your pick in the comments.**

---
facebook.image_caption: LeBron James is 41, still the center of the NBA universe, and the most important free agent of the 2026 offseason. Day 4 — and the Heat just entered the conversation.

article.headline: Day 4: Miami Heat Enter LeBron James Super Team Race

stories.title: Ja Morant Traded to Portland Trail Blazers

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: BREAKING: Ja Morant has been traded to the Portland Trail Blazers. Memphis gets Kris Murray and Jerami Grant. The Grizzlies rebuild is officially underway. Deal closes July 6. #TrailBlazers #Grizzlies #NBA

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Ja Morant — two-time All-Star and the face of Grizzlies basketball — is now a Portland Trail Blazer. Memphis enters full rebuild mode. This is a new era for both franchises. #TrailBlazers #Grizzlies

facebook.long_form:
The Memphis Grizzlies era of Ja Morant is officially over.

In one of the most significant trades of the 2026 NBA offseason, Memphis dealt two-time All-Star Ja Morant to the Portland Trail Blazers. The Grizzlies received Kris Murray and Jerami Grant in return — a package that signals Memphis is prioritizing assets and flexibility over competing in the short term. In related transactions, the Dallas Mavericks acquired Santi Aldama and draft rights to Tarik Biberovic, completing the multi-team framework.

For Memphis, this is a painful but necessary pivot. The Grizzlies built their identity around Morant's jaw-dropping athleticism and fearless style of play. He made them the league's most exciting young team. But rebuilds require sacrifice, and the Grizzlies are making the call to start fresh. What comes next in Memphis is one of the more fascinating storylines to follow this offseason.

For Portland, this is a franchise-altering moment. The Trail Blazers have not had a player this electric in years. Morant brings highlight-reel energy, superstar talent, and box-office appeal to a market that has been waiting for a reason to get loud again. The deal officially closes July 6. When it does, the NBA's Pacific Northwest chapter will look entirely different.

**Are you excited to see Ja Morant in Portland? Sound off below.**

---
facebook.image_caption: Ja Morant is a Portland Trail Blazer. The two-time All-Star leaves Memphis behind and begins a new chapter on the West Coast.

article.headline: Ja Morant Traded to Portland Trail Blazers as Memphis Enters Full Rebuild

stories.title: LaMelo Ball Heads to Minnesota Timberwolves

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text: TRADE: LaMelo Ball is heading to the Minnesota Timberwolves. The two-time All-Star joins Anthony Edwards in Minneapolis. Charlotte just traded the best player in franchise history. #Timberwolves #Hornets #NBA

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text: Anthony Edwards + LaMelo Ball in Minnesota. Let that sink in. Two of the most exciting players in basketball just became teammates. The Timberwolves are appointment TV this season. #Timberwolves #NBA

facebook.long_form:
The Minnesota Timberwolves just made themselves one of the most dangerous teams in the Western Conference.

Charlotte Hornets traded LaMelo Ball — the best player in franchise history — to Minnesota, pairing him with Anthony Edwards to create one of the most thrilling backcourts in basketball. Ball is a two-time All-Star who was selected No. 3 overall in the 2020 NBA Draft. His ability to make passes that no one else sees, combined with his three-point range and sheer star power, makes him the ideal complement to Edwards' relentless attacking style.

For Charlotte, this is a gut punch. LaMelo Ball is the franchise — the player who turned the Hornets into a watchable, exciting team with genuine energy behind him. Trading him means the Hornets are in some form of transition, looking to reshape their roster and direction. It is a bold move that will be debated in Charlotte for years.

For Minnesota, the calculus is clear: Edwards is ready to win, and management went out and got him a partner. The Timberwolves already have a strong defensive foundation and now add one of the most creative offensive players in the league. Western Conference teams should be paying close attention. The Wolves just became a problem.

**Edwards + LaMelo in Minnesota — how far do you think they go next season? Let us know in the comments.**

---
facebook.image_caption: LaMelo Ball to Minnesota. Two-time All-Star meets All-Star in a pairing that could define the Western Conference for years to come.

article.headline: LaMelo Ball Traded to Minnesota Timberwolves to Join Anthony Edwards

stories.title: Austin Reaves Signs $185M Deal with Lakers — Historic Undrafted Contract

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Austin Reaves just signed a 4-year, $185M deal with the Lakers — the largest contract ever for an undrafted NBA player. From undrafted out of Arkansas in 2021 to $185M. Lakers build around him now. #Lakers #NBA

facebook.long_form:
No one drafted him. Five years later, he just signed the biggest contract ever given to an undrafted NBA player.

Austin Reaves re-signed with the Los Angeles Lakers on a 4-year, $185 million deal — a number that no undrafted player in the history of the league has ever seen. Reaves came out of the University of Arkansas in 2021 without a single team calling his name on draft night. He earned every minute he played, built his game season by season, and made himself indispensable in one of the most scrutinized markets in professional sports.

With LeBron James departing, the Lakers need a new identity. Reaves is now the cornerstone of that identity. He will play alongside Walker Kessler as part of a Los Angeles team that is resetting, rebuilding, and looking to reestablish itself as a Western Conference force. The city of Los Angeles is behind him. The story behind him is undeniable.

This is the kind of contract that motivates every player who ever slipped through the draft-day cracks. Austin Reaves is proof that the work pays off — all $185 million worth of it.

**Is Austin Reaves worth $185 million to the Lakers? Share your take below.**

---
facebook.image_caption: $185 million for an undrafted player from Arkansas. Austin Reaves re-signs with the Lakers in the most historic undrafted contract in NBA history.

article.headline: Austin Reaves Signs $185M Lakers Deal — The Most Historic Undrafted Contract in NBA History

stories.title: AJ Dybantsa Signs Rookie Deal — No. 1 Pick's Journey Begins

x_posts.label: Post 5-1
x_posts.text: No. 1 pick AJ Dybantsa has signed his rookie deal with the Washington Wizards — 4 years, $66.91M. Pair him with Trae Young ($212M) and Washington is suddenly must-watch basketball. #Wizards #NBA

x_posts.label: Post 5-2
x_posts.text:
NBA free agency is absolutely wild right now:

- LeBron still unsigned (Heat, Warriors, Cavs all in)
- Ja Morant traded to Portland
- LaMelo Ball to Minnesota
- Reaves gets $185M historic deal
- Dybantsa signed in DC

The league is never boring. #NBA

facebook.long_form:
Washington has a new cornerstone — and so does the NBA's next generation.

AJ Dybantsa, the No. 1 overall pick in the 2026 NBA Draft, signed his 4-year, $66.91 million rookie scale contract with the Washington Wizards on July 2. The 6-foot-8.5 forward out of BYU arrives in the nation's capital as the most prized prospect in basketball, a player whose combination of size, skill, and versatility had scouts raving throughout his college career.

What makes Washington's rebuild especially compelling is the pairing Dybantsa steps into. The Wizards also signed Trae Young to a 4-year, $212 million deal. Young is one of the most prolific scorers and playmakers in basketball — a ball-handler who can create for himself and for others at an elite level. Imagine a 41-year-old LeBron having to choose between Miami's super team, Golden State's dynasty, Cleveland's nostalgia — and somewhere in the back of his mind, a young Wizards team building something real.

Together, Dybantsa and Young represent Washington's clearest path to relevance in years. The Wizards are investing nearly $279 million in their new cornerstones. They are not building to compete someday. They are building to compete soon.

**Are the Wizards officially back on the map? Tell us what you think below.**

---
facebook.image_caption: No. 1 pick AJ Dybantsa is officially a Washington Wizard. The future starts now in the nation's capital.

article.headline: AJ Dybantsa Signs Rookie Deal with Washington Wizards — No. 1 Pick's Journey Begins
